code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1, text analysis only

reliability.note: Proceeds with assumptions when input is vague; no active minimum-clarification protocol defined

performance_context.note: 357 lines; eight reference modules appropriately separate depth

agent_usability.note: Step 2 retrieval instruction shares the same web-access assumption issue as basic-discovery-translational-opportunity-finder

agent_specific.note: Reframing rules module is a strong differentiator; composability slightly constrained by full structured output mandate

inputs.label: 12-gene immune signature in ovarian cancer — best translational angle

assertions.text: Discovery type correctly classified before translational framing
Statistical significance not equated with translational value
Bridge evidence and validation burden assessed per path
Single best-fit translational framing stated with justification
No fabricated validation precedents or external cohort claims

inputs.label: scRNA-seq resistant macrophage state — translational opportunity assessment

assertions.text: Cell-state discovery type distinguished from signature or single-marker finding
Assayability and implementation feasibility assessed
Inflated 'therapeutic target' framing downgraded to 'target nomination hypothesis'
Main translation barriers identified
Mechanism-first framing recommended as safer alternative when bridge evidence is weak

inputs.label: Pathway-activity score positioning beyond pure mechanism

assertions.text: Reframing recommendation produced with 'framing to avoid' and 'recommended framing'
Internal validation not presented as external validation
Narrowest credible publication-grade version identified
No patient-specific use-case recommended
Self-critical risk review present

inputs.label: Methylation classifier — framing as diagnosis, prognosis, or treatment-response prediction

assertions.text: Three competing framings compared with explicit evidence assessment
Cross-platform transferability and feature stability assessed
Computational association not implied as clinical readiness
Discovery type classified correctly as genomic alteration pattern
Workflow placement in clinical setting assessed

inputs.label: TCGA-derived risk model — narrowest defensible translational topic

assertions.text: TCGA internal-only training correctly flagged as requiring external validation before translational claims
Comparator burden and assay transfer burden assessed
Narrowest defensible framing preferred over most impressive-sounding framing
Primary next-step direction specific and actionable
No fabricated TCGA metadata or external cohort availability

key_strengths: Reframing rules module is a unique and powerful differentiator that enforces conservative translational claim calibration
Discovery type classification before translational framing prevents category confusion (single marker vs signature vs cell state)
Twelve hard rules explicitly prevent equating statistical association with clinical utility
Strong self-critical review mandate in Step 8 surfaces overclaim risks before output is finalized

recommendations.title: Step 2 literature retrieval creates inconsistency without live web access
recommendations.problem: Step 2 requires retrieving peer-reviewed literature to define disease-discovery intersection before framing, but offline execution defaults to training memory, which may introduce unreliable or outdated evidence claims.
recommendations.root_cause: No fallback behavior defined for environments without web search capability.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit fallback: label all evidence in Section B as 'based on training knowledge — user should verify with current literature' when live retrieval is not available.

recommendations.title: No structured clarification prompt for ambiguous discovery inputs
recommendations.problem: When only a gene name or pathway is given without disease context or data modality, the skill proceeds with stated assumptions rather than asking targeted follow-up questions.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section lists examples but does not define when clarification is required before proceeding.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum clarification check: if data modality or disease context is missing, ask 2–3 focused questions before executing Step 3 onwards.
